name: The Trojan War
shortDescription: The Trojan War was a legendary conflict between the Achaeans (Greeks) and the city of Troy, sparked by the abduction of Helen. It is a central event in Greek mythology and literature.
description: The Trojan War is one of the most famous and important events in Greek mythology. It was a decade-long siege of the city of Troy (Ilium) by a coalition of Achaean (Greek) kingdoms. The war is traditionally said to have begun after Paris, a Trojan prince, abducted Helen, wife of Menelaus, king of Sparta. Menelaus appealed to his brother Agamemnon, king of Mycenae, who gathered a large army from across Greece to sail to Troy and retrieve Helen. The Achaean forces included legendary heroes such as Achilles, Odysseus, Ajax, and Nestor. The siege of Troy was long and arduous, marked by numerous battles, heroic deeds, and divine interventions. The gods of Olympus took sides in the conflict, with some favoring the Achaeans (Hera, Athena, Poseidon) and others supporting the Trojans (Aphrodite, Apollo, Ares). Key episodes include the quarrel between Achilles and Agamemnon, the death of Hector at the hands of Achilles, and the construction of the Trojan Horse, a giant wooden horse concealing Achaean soldiers. The Achaeans feigned retreat, leaving the horse as a gift. The Trojans, unaware of the trick, brought the horse inside their city walls. That night, the Achaean soldiers emerged from the horse, opened the city gates, and allowed the Achaean army to enter and sack Troy. The Trojan War represents themes of honor, glory, love, betrayal, and the destructive nature of war. Its impact on Greek culture and literature is immense, inspiring countless works of art, poetry, and drama.
